questions.question: Explain the concept of a perceptron and its role in the architecture of neural networks.
keyPoints: Perceptron as a single artificial neuron
Weighted sum of inputs plus bias

questions.question: Describe the bias‑variance tradeoff and its relationship to overfitting and underfitting in machine learning models.
keyPoints: Bias: error from overly simplistic assumptions
Variance: error from excessive model complexity
Overfitting (high variance) vs underfitting (high bias) and common remedies

questions.question: Discuss the architecture of a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and explain how it is trained for image classification tasks. Include details on the role of convolutional, pooling, and fully‑connected layers, the back‑propagation process, and mention a popular deep learning framework (e.g., TensorFlow or PyTorch) used for implementation.
keyPoints: Convolutional layer: filters, feature maps, stride, padding
Training: loss function (cross‑entropy), optimizer (SGD/Adam), back‑propagation through layers, and example code snippet in TensorFlow/PyTorch

questions.question: Evaluate the performance of deep learning models using appropriate metrics. Explain accuracy, precision, recall, F1‑score, and discuss when each metric is suitable. Additionally, describe techniques to mitigate overfitting, such as dropout, L1/L2 regularization, data augmentation, and early stopping.
keyPoints: Accuracy: overall correct predictions; suitable when classes are balanced
Precision: true positives/(true positives + false positives); useful for imbalanced classes
Recall: true positives/(true positives + false negatives); critical when missing a positive is costly
F1‑score: harmonic mean of precision and recall; balances precision and recall; overfitting mitigation: dropout, L1/L2, data augmentation, early stopping
